subject: Computer Networking
title: Security Monitoring and Logging
description: One-minute skill: Security Monitoring and Logging.

hook.text: If a tree falls in the forest with no one around, does it make a sound? If a breach happens with no logs, did it happen?

buildup.text: Logs record every login, access, change, and error. They're the forensic trail investigators follow after a breach.

discovery.text: SIEM tools aggregate logs from firewalls, servers, and apps into one dashboard. Correlations reveal hidden patterns.

twist.text: Attackers often delete logs to cover their tracks. Sending logs to a separate, hardened system defeats that tactic.

climax.text: Real-time alerting catches suspicious activity as it happens — a login from two countries in ten minutes, for example.

punchline.text: You can't protect what you can't see. Logs are your eyes.

quiz.question: Why should logs be stored on a separate system?
options: To prevent attackers from deleting them to cover their tracks
To make logs load faster in a browser
To reduce the cost of storage
